# pi2s3

> pi2s3 ("Pi to S3") takes a block-level image of a Linux machine's disk — a
> Raspberry Pi by design — and streams it to AWS S3 with partclone. Restore a
> complete, bootable machine to new hardware with one command. Two bash scripts,
> no daemons, no build step. Zero-downtime database quiesce built in.

## For AI agents: "backup my site with pi2s3"

Run these ON the host being backed up (it images local disks; SSH in if remote).
Requires Linux, root/sudo, and working AWS credentials.

1. Verify host: `uname -s` is `Linux`; a local disk exists (`lsblk`).
2. Already set up? `test -f ~/pi2s3/config.env && echo yes` — if yes, go to step 5.
3. Check AWS creds: `aws sts get-caller-identity`. If it fails, ask the user for
   AWS credentials, an S3 bucket name, and a region. Never guess these.
4. Install unattended:
   `git clone https://github.com/andrewbakercloudscale/pi2s3.git ~/pi2s3`
   `cd ~/pi2s3 && S3_BUCKET=<bucket> S3_REGION=<region> PI2S3_YES=1 bash install.sh`
5. Back up: `bash ~/pi2s3/pi-image-backup.sh --force`
6. Verify: `bash ~/pi2s3/pi-image-backup.sh --verify` then report date + size.

Databases are quiesced automatically with zero downtime (MySQL/MariaDB via
`SET GLOBAL read_only=ON`; PostgreSQL via `CHECKPOINT`), in Docker or native.
Never run `pi-image-restore.sh` unless explicitly asked — it overwrites a disk.

## Key commands

- `bash pi-image-backup.sh --force` — run a backup now
- `bash pi-image-backup.sh --dry-run` — show the plan, upload nothing
- `bash pi-image-backup.sh --db-check` — confirm DB detection + zero-downtime quiesce (no imaging)
- `bash pi-image-backup.sh --list` / `--verify` — list / verify S3 backups
- `bash pi-image-backup.sh --cost` — S3 usage and estimated monthly cost
- `bash install.sh --iam-policy` — print the minimum AWS IAM policy
- `bash pi-image-restore.sh` — restore to new hardware (destructive; ask first)

## Docs

- Full agent runbook: https://github.com/andrewbakercloudscale/pi2s3/blob/main/AGENTS.md
- README: https://github.com/andrewbakercloudscale/pi2s3/blob/main/README.md
- Website: https://pi2s3.com
- Setup: https://pi2s3.com/setup.html
- Reference & config: https://pi2s3.com/reference.html
- Recovery: https://pi2s3.com/recovery.html
